rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (verifying the reduce_scatter param in the test), so recall is low for both. X does deeper, verified technical reasoning (confirms DPMetadata construction, device_communicator semantics, group cleanup/destroy safety, and the _DP condition change are all correct against actual vLLM source) and gives one grounded, actionable nitpick with file/line. Y's output is a raw agent trace (fetch/gate/review steps, 'Maximum iterations reached' blocker, redundant 'Sweep:' bullets) rather than a synthesized review, and its three findings are all docstring-nit level, one of which (enable_expert_parallel) isn't verifiable from the shown diff — lower precision and much lower signal-to-noise despite structured file/line tags.
